After merge+deploy: navigate to /calibration — verify both trading and sports domain panels render without 500
Trigger /api/cron/weather-tick manually with CRON_SECRET bearer token — verify one row appears in weather_log table
Trigger /api/cron/mood-prompt — verify Telegram message arrives asking 1-5 energy rating
Reply to mood prompt with a number (e.g. '4') — verify row appears in mood_log with energy=4
Check agent_events table for any error rows from the new cron routes after first automated run
Verify all 7 new crons appear in the Vercel dashboard Cron Jobs tab after deploy (Vercel Hobby allows max 1 hourly — weather-tick is hourly; confirm Hobby plan allows it or upgrade needed)
unknowns: Vercel Hobby plan cron limit: weather-tick runs every hour (`0 * * * *`). Hobby plan historically limits hourly crons to 1. There is already no other hourly cron in vercel.json before Sprint 10, so weather-tick should be the only hourly cron — but this should be verified in the Vercel dashboard after deploy.
The ODDS_API_KEY env var is required by sports-picks-scan but its presence in Vercel env was not verified in this session. If the key is missing, sports-picks-scan will return 500 on first run.
TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var is required by the mood-response webhook. Its presence in Vercel env was not verified (it appears in .env.local key list but value was not confirmed).

The sports-picks-scan uses the `predictions` table (AIPE, migration 0142) while the existing sports-results cron uses the older `sports_picks` table. The new sports-results-FETCH cron also reads from `predictions`. These two tables serve different purposes — the split is intentional but should be confirmed with Colin before going live.
